titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/248

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=224 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:09:48.673133+00 | translation moa=225 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:09:54.987278+00

div.p:
W. miseratione diuina Ecclesie . Sancti Andree minister Humilis omnibus has litteras uisuris uel audituris
eternam in domino Salutem. Ad uniuersitatis uestre noticiam uolumus deuenire . nos de communi assensu ⁊ uolun
tate Capituli nostri . ⁊ archidiaconorum nostrorum . dedisse . ⁊ concessisse . abbati . ⁊ canonicis de Sancta cruce
et eorum successoribus in perpetuum in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam . totam medietatem . Decime Bladi pertinentis ad
ecclesiam de Kyngor ad sustentationem predictorum canonicorum et pauperum . et peregrinorum. Quare uo
lumus ut predicti canonici et eorum successores easdem decimas teneant et possideant in perpetuum nomine sim
plicis beneficii . adeo Libere . integre . et honorifice . sicut aliquod simplex benefitium in toto regno Scotie . liberius
quietius ⁊ honorificentius tenetur et possidetur. Et ut hec nostra concessio rata ⁊ inconcussa perseueret [ms. pererseueret]  eam
presentis pagine testimonio . ⁊ sigilli nostri ⁊ sigillorum Capituli ⁊ archidiaconorum nostrorum appositione corrobora
uimus. Hiis testibus . magistro Laurentio Archidiacono . Sancti Andree . W . de Bosco . Archidiacono . Laodon’ . Rad’ nigero . decano
Laodon’ . Rad’. decano de Karel . Magistro . alexandro de Sancto martino . Magistro . Dauid de Bernham . Symone de
Noisi . Willelmo . de Golin . Symone capellano domini episcopi . Ricardo de Kalcou . clerico . Iohanne clerico domini episcopi . ⁊ multis aliis

p.cl: William by divince mercy humble servant of the church of St Andrews to all who shall view or hear these letters, Eternal Salvation in the Lord:

We wish to bring to your attention of you all that we have, by the common agreement and will of our chapter and of our archdeacons, given and granted to the abbot and canons of Holyrodd and their successors in perpetuity, in pure and perpetual alms, the whole moiety of the teind of corn pertaining to the church of Kinghorn for the support of the aforesaid canons, paupers and pilgrims. Wherefore we will that the aforesaid canons and their successors should hold and possess the same teinds in perpetuity as a simple benefice, as freely, wholly, and honourably as any simple benefice in the whold kingdom of Scotia is freely, quietly and honourably held and possessed.
And that this our grant might endure sure and unbroken, we have secured it by the testimony of this document, and by the application of our own seal and the seals of the Chapter and our archdeacons.

With these witnesses, Master Laurence the archdeacon of St Andrews, William del Bois the archdeacon of Lothian, Ralph Niger the dean of Lothian, Ralph the dean of Crail, Master Alexander de Saint-Martin, Master David of Bernham, Simon de Noisy, William of Gullane, Simon the chaplain of the lord bishop, Richard of Kelso the clerk, John the clerk of the lord Bishop, and many others.
